text.headline: Ink & Type
text.text: Five centuries of printing, from Gutenberg's workshop to the World Wide Web.

text.headline: Gutenberg's Movable Type
text.text: Johannes Gutenberg introduces a practical movable-type printing press in Mainz, making the mass production of books possible for the first time in Europe.

text.headline: The First Newspaper
text.text: Relation aller Fürnemmen und gedenckwürdigen Historien, published in Strasbourg, is widely regarded as the world's first newspaper.

text.headline: The Steam Press
text.text: The Times of London adopts a steam-powered printing press, quadrupling the speed at which a newspaper can be printed.

text.headline: The Penny Press
text.text: The New York Sun launches at one cent a copy, pioneering a mass-circulation model aimed at ordinary readers rather than the elite.

text.headline: The Linotype Machine
text.text: Ottmar Mergenthaler's Linotype mechanizes typesetting, casting an entire line of type at once and transforming newspaper production.

text.headline: The Web Goes Public
text.text: CERN places the World Wide Web into the public domain, opening the door to a new era of digital publishing.
